page: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. It features 128 virtual CPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 48 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 1024.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core.

In benchmark testing, the E128as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Its CPU capabilities are highlighted by top 10% scores in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as PassMark CPU evaluations. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 12383.33 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 376.87 GB/sec. Database and key-value operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by a Redis SET throughput of 177.27 million operations per second and top-tier PassMark database operations.

This server does not include local storage, though it is Premium Storage capable, meaning external storage must be attached. It also does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of integrated local NVMe storage represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space, but the high memory capacity mitigates this for in-memory processing.

Given its massive memory footprint and dedicated high-frequency AMD EPYC cores, the E128as_v7 offers high resource density for memory-intensive enterprise applications. It is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, heavy data processing pipelines, and medium-to-large LLM inference tasks, where it achieves top-tier text generation and prompt processing speeds.
description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz. It features 1024.0 GB of RAM, establishing an 8.0 GB per core memory-to-vCPU ratio. The server lacks local storage but is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. In benchmark evaluations, the E128as_v7 delivers top-tier performance, including top 10% results in multi-core CPU execution, memory bandwidth, and Redis operations. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, such as large-scale in-memory databases, data analytics pipelines, and LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized server featuring 128 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz, and 1024 GB of RAM, designed for high-throughput database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E128as_v7 is a memory-optimized server with 128 vCPUs, a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, and 1024 GB of RAM for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency dedicated processor paired with massive memory capacity for demanding in-memory database and enterprise data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor
1024 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and Redis performance benchmarks
Premium Storage capable configuration without bundled local disks
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and LLM inference
